basic.routeDesc: HTTP method and path for this endpoint
basic.sourceDesc: Where to get or write data
basic.authDesc: How clients authenticate to this endpoint

cache.title: Cache
cache.desc: Cache GET responses to reduce database load
cache.smartInvalidationDesc: Automatically clear cache when data in the source table changes

rateLimit.title: Rate Limit
rateLimit.desc: Limit requests per IP to prevent abuse

tester.title: API Tester
tester.noParams: No query parameters. Click + Add to add one.
tester.unsavedWarning: You have unsaved changes. Save first to test the latest version of this endpoint.
tester.noResult: Send a request to see the response

responseConfig: Response configuration (field selection, renaming, FK populate) will be available in the next update. The endpoint will return all fields by default.

request.title: Request Schema
request.mustProvide: Must be provided in request body
request.createHint: POST request body must contain all required fields as JSON. System fields (id, created_at, updated_at) are auto-generated and will be ignored if sent.
request.updateHint: PUT/PATCH request body contains only the fields you want to update. System fields are ignored. The record ID must be provided as a URL parameter or query param (?id=...).
request.deleteHint: DELETE request requires the record ID as a URL parameter (/path/:id) or query param (?id=...). No request body needed.
request.findHint: GET request accepts query parameters for pagination, sorting and filtering. Use filter[column]=value to filter results. No request body needed.
request.findOneHint: GET request requires the search value as a URL parameter (e.g. /users/:email). The column used for lookup is configured via "Search by" in the Basic tab.
request.customSqlHint: For custom SQL endpoints, parameters are passed as {{param}} placeholders in the query. They can come from URL path params, query string, or request body.
request.sqlParamsDesc: Parameters detected in your SQL query. Values are taken from URL path, query string, or request body.
request.sqlNoParams: No parameters detected. Add {{param}} placeholders to your SQL query.
request.sqlHowParamsDesc1: Use {{name}} in your SQL — it becomes a safe parameterized query ($1, $2...).
request.sqlHowParamsDesc2: If the parameter name matches a :param in the URL path — it's taken from the path.
request.sqlHowParamsDesc3: Otherwise it's taken from query string (?name=value) or JSON body.
request.sqlHowParamsDesc4: All parameters are required — missing values will return 400 error.
request.selectTableFirst: Select a table source in the Basic tab to see the request schema.
request.searchParamDesc: Search by {{column}} — value from URL path
request.filtersDesc: Add filters as query parameters to narrow results. Format: filter[column]=value or filter[column][operator]=value
request.filterExIn: One of values (status IN active, pending)
request.filtersMultiDesc: Combine multiple filters — all conditions are joined with AND (all must match)
request.filtersOps: Operators: eq, neq, gt, gte, lt, lte, like, ilike, in, is_null

response.fieldSelectionDesc: Choose which fields to include in the API response. Use alias to rename fields (e.g. created_at -> createdAt).
response.fkPopulateDesc: Automatically resolve and include related records for foreign key columns.
response.selectTableFirst: Select a table source in the Basic tab to configure response fields.
response.sqlResponseDesc: The response contains the raw result of your SQL query. The structure depends on your SELECT statement.
response.sqlResponseSelectHint: SELECT queries return an array of row objects with column names as keys.
response.sqlResponseInsertHint: INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE queries return the affected rows (if RETURNING is used) or a count of affected rows.
response.sqlResponseTip: Use RETURNING * in INSERT/UPDATE to get the full row back in the response.
response.errorsDesc: Possible error responses for this endpoint
response.headersDesc: Headers included in every response from this endpoint

deleteConfirm.title: Delete Endpoint
deleteConfirm.desc: Are you sure? This endpoint will stop responding to requests.

graphql.title: GraphQL Playground
graphql.noResult: Run a query to see results
graphql.noTables: No tables found in this project.
graphql.docsQueriesDesc: Each table has two query fields: list (with limit, offset, where filters) and getById.
graphql.docsMutationsDesc: Each table has create, update, and delete mutations. ID, created_at, updated_at are auto-managed.
graphql.docsAuthDesc: The GraphQL endpoint respects the authentication setting configured in your project settings. Use session, API token, or public access.
graphql.authBanner: Authentication: API Key required. Pass via X-API-Key header.

websocket.title: WebSocket
websocket.authInfo: Authentication: API Key required in connection URL query parameter or header.
websocket.noTables: No tables found in this project.
websocket.exampleDesc: This is what a WebSocket message looks like when a subscribed event occurs:

swagger.title: API Documentation
swagger.description: Auto-generated API documentation from your endpoints. Download the OpenAPI spec to use with Swagger UI, Postman, or any OpenAPI-compatible tool.
swagger.noEndpoints: No API endpoints found. Create endpoints first to generate documentation.
